refinement_notes.note: Replace the custom SVG score renderer with OpenSheetMusicDisplay using MusicXML as the notation contract; preserve responsive browser rendering and accessible states.

Prioritize native OSMD/Guitar Pro visual grammar over the existing site theme. Replace free-form chord and section text with MusicXML harmony and rehearsal semantics, use neutral score typography, and restyle the score player as a dedicated notation workspace.
Replace OSMD and MusicXML rendering with alphaTab using alphaTex, GuitarPro notation mode, native standard-notation plus TAB layout, and alphaTab's cursor/player API surface.
Convert Songs to a chord-only alphaTab chart: render a single standard staff with chord symbols and bar structure, use empty beats instead of rests or notes, remove tablature and playback controls.
Add an in-page chord-font selector to the alphaTab chord chart so users can switch among suitable font treatments and immediately compare the re-rendered score.

Add a dedicated music-font style lab page with a symbol-rich standard-notation and TAB alphaTab demo, and switchable Bravura, Petaluma, Gootville/Gonville, Finale Maestro, MScore/Emmentaler, and Leland SMuFL fonts using font-specific metadata.
Make Finale Maestro the default engraving font and add a separate chord-name font selector for Edwin, JazzCord, and Campania on the music font lab.
Finalize Finale Maestro plus Edwin for production Songs chord charts, remove the experimental chord-font selector from Songs, and retain the style lab for reference.
Remove all left-side staff and track labels from production chord charts so every score system starts at the same horizontal origin and measure columns remain aligned across rows.

Simplify production song detail pages to title, artist, and score only: remove duplicate alphaTab score title and artist metadata, remove descriptive metadata summary, and remove all Chord Chart / Finale Maestro / Edwin header chrome above the score.
Force every production score system, including a short final system, to occupy the same full available width with matching left and right boundaries.
Increase vertical separation between the initial tempo marking and the first chord name so they never collide, while preserving the simple title-artist-score layout and aligned systems.

Hide all measure numbers, move chord names slightly lower while retaining a readable gap above the staff, and replace Edwin with a slightly larger Google Fonts Charm typeface in production song charts.

Add a production chord-font comparison selector for Libre Baskerville, DM Serif Display, Literata (interpreting the requested Liberata), DM Serif Text, and Scheherazade New, with a larger readable chord size and preserved score alignment.

Finalize Scheherazade New as the production chord font, remove the comparison selector, and left-align chord names at their beat/bar anchor so long first chords never extend left of a measure boundary.
Render four measures per system on mobile as well as desktop, scaling the notation to the available phone width without horizontal overflow while preserving equal system widths and column alignment.

Reduce excessive horizontal padding around production song scores on mobile so four-measure systems use substantially more of the phone width, while preserving readable title spacing and desktop layout.
Remove alphaTab's internal 35-unit horizontal page padding on mobile, since the surrounding page gutter is already minimal; keep vertical notation padding and desktop engraving margins unchanged.

Align the entire production song page, including title and alphaTab score, to the same responsive horizontal gutter and container system used by the global header on both mobile and desktop; remove page-specific gutter values and redundant inner score padding.

history.event: Initialized run from docs/ideas/guitar-score-web-renderer.md

Design Agent produced design.json after desktop browser baseline inspection
Builder implemented typed TAB events, responsive score rows, and visual playback controls
Verifier passed typecheck, lint, production build, desktop browser checks, narrow browser checks, and playback interaction
Design Critic approved with one low-severity future audio follow-up
Code Critic approved with no findings; Supervisor marked the run done
